observation_summary: The JsonReader fails to parse unquoted numeric keys in JSON maps, throwing an IllegalStateException because it expects a number but encounters an unquoted string.
hypothesis: The JsonReader's nextLong() and nextInt() methods were missing logic to handle PEEKED_UNQUOTED tokens, causing them to fall through to an error-throwing branch instead of attempting to parse the unquoted string as a number.
prediction: The code will be updated to include PEEKED_UNQUOTED in the conditional checks within nextLong() and nextInt(), and will call nextUnquotedValue() to retrieve the string for parsing.
experiment_rationale: The provided diff shows that the conditional logic in both nextLong() and nextInt() was expanded to include PEEKED_UNQUOTED and now correctly invokes nextUnquotedValue() to extract the value, confirming the hypothesis that the parsing algorithm was incomplete.
reasoning_summary: The defect is an algorithmic omission in the parsing procedure. The code failed to account for a specific token type (unquoted strings) when attempting to read numeric values. This is a procedural logic error rather than a missing guard (Checking) or a wrong constant (Assignment/Initialization).

alternative_types.why_not_primary: While the fix involves adding a condition, the root cause is that the parsing algorithm itself was incomplete for the supported input format (unquoted keys), not that a validation check was missing.
